pathways.label: Court-ordered expunction - 943.0585
pathways.summary: Florida expunction is generally for cases that did not end in adjudication of guilt and meet strict statutory criteria. A person may be eligible to petition for expunction if no charging document was filed, or if a charging document was filed but the case was dismissed, nolle prosequi, dismissed by the court, resolved by judgment of acquittal, or ended in a not-guilty verdict. The record must also not be ineligible under 943.0584.…
ruleClauses: Florida expunction is generally for cases that did not end in adjudication of guilt and meet strict statutory criteria.

A person may be eligible to petition for expunction if no charging document was filed, or if a charging document was filed but the case was dismissed, nolle prosequi, dismissed by the court, resolved by judgment of acquittal, or ended in a not-guilty verdict. The record must also not be ineligible under 943.0584. ("https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0585.html")
Disposition No charge filed, dismissed, nolle prosequi, acquittal, judgment of acquittal, or not guilty

Prior adjudication check User must not have disqualifying adjudications
Same-case adjudication check User must not have been adjudicated guilty/delinquent for acts from the arrest/incident
Supervision User must no longer be under court supervision for the case
Prior Florida seal/expunge Generally no prior Florida court-ordered seal/expunge
Certificate FDLE Certificate of Eligibility required before filing
Court discretion Court may deny even if statutorily eligible

pathways.label: Automatic sealing - 943.0595
pathways.summary: Florida has an automatic sealing pathway for certain records that end favorably. FDLE automatically seals qualifying records after receiving the certified disposition from the clerk. Automatic sealing may apply when:
ruleClauses: Florida has an automatic sealing pathway for certain records that end favorably. FDLE automatically seals qualifying records after receiving the certified disposition from the clerk.
Automatic sealing may apply when:
No charging document filed Eligible if not excluded
Charging document filed, all counts dismissed/nolle prossed/dismissed by court Eligible if not excluded
Not guilty verdict on all counts Eligible, unless not guilty by reason of insanity
Judgment of acquittal on all counts Eligible

There is no limit on the number of automatic sealings under 943.0595, but automatic sealing does not require other criminal justice agencies to surrender or seal their own copies the same way a full court-ordered sealing/expungement order may. (
"https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&Search_String=&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0595.html")

Wilma rule: Automatic sealing is helpful, but do not substitute it blindly for court-ordered expunction. If the user wants maximum cleanup and qualifies for expunction, route to FDLE certificate + court petition.
exclusionRules: No charging document filed Eligible if not excluded
Charging document filed, all counts dismissed/nolle prossed/dismissed by court Eligible if not excluded

pathways.label: Human trafficking victim expunction - 943.0583
pathways.summary: This is Floridas broadest special expunction route. A human trafficking victim may petition to expunge criminal history records from arrests or charges for one or more offenses committed or reported while they were a victim and as part of the trafficking scheme or at the direction of a trafficker. This route applies without regard to the disposition of the arrest or charges, meaning it can reach some convictions that normal sealing/expungement cannot.…
ruleClauses: This is Floridas broadest special expunction route.

without regard to the disposition

Florida Record Sealing & Expunction
A complete operational knowledge base for clearing criminal records in Florida: the FDLE Certificate-of-Eligibility
and the administrative, lawful-self-defense, human-trafficking, and juvenile relief routes — all under Chapter
Primary statutes § 943.059 (sealing); § 943.0585 (expunction); § 943.0584 (disqualifying offenses); § 943.0595
Florida draws two lines the agent must never blur. First, SEALING (§ 943.059) hides a record from the public while government
agencies still see it; EXPUNCTION (§ 943.0585) destroys it (FDLE keeps only a confidential notation) and the person may
generally deny it occurred. Second, almost all relief is a TWO-STEP process: the person must first obtain a Certificate of
Eligibility from FDLE ($75, valid 12 months), and THEN petition the court — and the certificate does NOT guarantee the court
will grant the order. Relief is generally ONCE PER LIFETIME. The disqualifying-offense list in § 943.0584 bars relief even
when adjudication was withheld. The agent must verify the current statute, the § 943.0584 list, and eligibility before relying.
Florida clears adult records by SEALING or EXPUNCTION, and the agent routes by disposition and goal. The
can be SEALED; if all charges were DISMISSED / dropped / not filed (and the state attorney certifies for
expunction), the record can be EXPUNGED. The agent should (1) confirm no adjudication of guilt and no prior
seal/expunge; (2) check the § 943.0584 disqualifier list; (3) determine seal vs. expunge by disposition; and (4)
run the FDLE Certificate-of-Eligibility step before the court petition.

The signature waiting period is the 10-year bridge: a record initially ineligible for expunction (because adjudication was withheld) may become expungeable after it has been SEALED for 10 years.
waitingPeriodRules.anchor: it has been SEALED for 10 years

Florida has a long list of offenses that are ineligible for court-ordered sealing or expunction when the record is a conviction for one of those offenses.

Important Florida nuance: for 943.0584, conviction includes a guilty/no-contest plea or determination of guilt even if adjudication is withheld. ("https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0584.html") Wilma should flag likely ineligible/manual review for: sexual misconduct; illegal explosives; terrorism; murder, manslaughter, homicide; domestic assault/battery; aggravated assault; felony battery, domestic battery by strangulation, aggravated battery; stalking/aggravated stalking; luring or enticing a child; human trafficking as a charged offense under the barred-offense list, unless the user is the trafficking victim seeking the separate victim route; kidnapping/false imprisonment; chapter 794 sexual offenses; lewd/lascivious offenses involving minors; arson; burglary of a dwelling; voyeurism/digital voyeurism; robbery, carjacking, home-invasion robbery; Florida Communications Fraud Act violations; elderly/disabled adult abuse; child abuse/aggravated child abuse; sexual performance by a child; public-corruption offenses under chapter 839; certain obscenity/computer sex crimes; selling/buying minors; aircraft piracy; manufacturing controlled substances; drug trafficking; any predicate offense for sexual predator or sexual offender registration. ("https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0584.html") Wilma rule: If a user says the judge withheld adjudication, do not assume eligibility.

A person may be eligible to petition for expunction if no charging document was filed, or if a charging document was filed but the case was dismissed, nolle prosequi, dismissed by the court, resolved by judgment of acquittal, or ended in a not-guilty verdict. The record must also not be ineligible under 943.0584. ("https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0585.html")

FDLE says a Personal Review lets a person examine their Florida criminal history for accuracy and completeness and is generally used to determine which arrest dates may be eligible. FDLE states no charge is assessed for that personal review service. ("https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/seal-and-expunge-process/frequently-asked-questions")

For normal sealing/expunction, the user submits the FDLE Certificate of Eligibility application with certified disposition, fingerprints, notarized signature, and $75 fee. Expunction also requires the prosecutor certified-statement page. ("https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/seal-and-expunge-process/certificate-of-eligibility-instructions")

After FDLE issues the certificate, the user files the petition, sworn statement/affidavit, certificate, and proposed order in the court with jurisdiction over the arrest, usually the county where the arrest occurred. FDLE says the certificate is not the final step and does not guarantee sealing or expunction. ("https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/seal-and-expunge-process/frequently-asked-questions")

Florida Record Sealing & Expunction
A complete operational knowledge base for clearing criminal records in Florida: the FDLE Certificate-of-Eligibility
process, court-ordered SEALING (§ 943.059) and EXPUNCTION (§ 943.0585), automatic sealing (§ 943.0595),
Coverage State of Florida (FDLE for the Certificate of Eligibility; the circuit/county court where the arrest
Primary statutes § 943.059 (sealing); § 943.0585 (expunction); § 943.0584 (disqualifying offenses); § 943.0595
Controlling changes 2019 amendment grouping same-incident arrests; 2023 HB 593 automatic-sealing expansion (§
Currency Reflects law as of mid-2026, including the automatic-sealing expansion
Florida draws two lines the agent must never blur. First, SEALING (§ 943.059) hides a record from the public while government
generally deny it occurred. Second, almost all relief is a TWO-STEP process: the person must first obtain a Certificate of
Eligibility from FDLE ($75, valid 12 months), and THEN petition the court — and the certificate does NOT guarantee the court
will grant the order. Relief is generally ONCE PER LIFETIME. The disqualifying-offense list in § 943.0584 bars relief even

can be SEALED; if all charges were DISMISSED / dropped / not filed (and the state attorney certifies for

sourceSections.text:
Users case outcome First Wilma route Treatment
Arrest, no charge filed Court-ordered expunction or automatic sealing Expunction possible if otherwise eligible; automatic sealing may also apply
Dismissed / nolle prosequi Court-ordered expunction or automatic sealing Expunction possible if otherwise eligible
Acquittal / not guilty Court-ordered expunction or automatic sealing Expunction possible if otherwise eligible
Judgment of acquittal Court-ordered expunction or automatic sealing Expunction possible if otherwise eligible
Withhold of adjudication Court-ordered sealing Sealing may be possible if offense is not barred and no other disqualifier applies
Adjudicated guilty adult conviction Usually no normal sealing/expunction Check special routes only, especially human trafficking
Prior sealing for same record for 10+ years Possible later expunction Only for the same record and only if otherwise eligible
Human trafficking survivor record Human trafficking victim expunction Can cover arrests, charges, and some convictions tied to trafficking
Lawful self-defense dismissal/no-file Lawful self-defense expunction Requires prosecutor certification

Juvenile record, age 18-20 Early juvenile expunction check Separate FDLE route
Arrest made by mistake / contrary to law Administrative expunction Usually through FDLE with arresting agency/state attorney endorsement

sourceSections.title: Remedy definitions Wilma must understand
sourceSections.text:
Sealing
A sealed Florida record is hidden from public access, but it is not destroyed. Certain government and licensing entities may still access sealed information. FDLE explains that when a record is sealed or expunged, the public will not have access, but certain government-related entities still have access to sealed records in full. (

"https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/seal-and-expunge-process/frequently-asked-questions")
Expunction
Expunction is stronger. Most agencies must physically destroy or obliterate the record, but FDLE retains a confidential copy. For most ordinary expungements, agencies that would have access to sealed records are generally told that a record was expunged but cannot see the record itself without a court order. ("https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0585.html")
Wilma rule: In Florida, do not say
gone forever.
Say:
expunged from public access and destroyed/obliterated by most agencies, while FDLE keeps a confidential copy.

sourceSections.title: Official forms Wilma should know
sourceSections.text:
Route Required document/form
Normal court sealing or expunction FDLE Application for Certificate of Eligibility
Expunction FDLE application plus Written Certified Statement from State Attorney/Statewide Prosecutor
Court filing after FDLE certificate Petition to Seal or Expunge, Sworn Statement/Affidavit, and Proposed Order
Human trafficking Human trafficking victim expunction petition, sworn statement, supporting documentation if available
Lawful self-defense FDLE Lawful Self-Defense Expungement application/certificate
Juvenile diversion FDLE Juvenile Diversion Expungement application
Early juvenile FDLE Early Juvenile Expungement application
Administrative mistaken arrest FDLE administrative expunction process
FDLE says the Certificate of Eligibility application must include identity/arrest information, be signed before a notary or deputy clerk, include certified dispositions, fingerprints, and a $75 nonrefundable processing fee. Expunction applications also require the State Attorney/Statewide Prosecutor certified-statement page. (

"https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/seal-and-expunge-process/certificate-of-eligibility-instructions")

sourceSections.title: Court-ordered expunction - 943.0585
sourceSections.text:
Florida expunction is generally for cases that did not end in adjudication of guilt and meet strict statutory criteria.
A person may be eligible to petition for expunction if no charging document was filed, or if a charging document was filed but the case was dismissed, nolle prosequi, dismissed by the court, resolved by judgment of acquittal, or ended in a not-guilty verdict. The record must also not be ineligible under 943.0584. ("https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0585.html")
Wilma must confirm:

Requirement Rule
Disposition No charge filed, dismissed, nolle prosequi, acquittal, judgment of acquittal, or not guilty
Barred offense check Must not be ineligible under 943.0584
Prior adjudication check User must not have disqualifying adjudications
Same-case adjudication check User must not have been adjudicated guilty/delinquent for acts from the arrest/incident
Supervision User must no longer be under court supervision for the case
Prior Florida seal/expunge Generally no prior Florida court-ordered seal/expunge
Certificate FDLE Certificate of Eligibility required before filing
Court discretion Court may deny even if statutorily eligible
For expunction, the petition must include a valid FDLE certificate and a sworn statement that the person satisfies the requirements and has no other pending seal/expunge petition. A certificate is valid for 12 months after FDLE issues it. (

"https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0585.html")

sourceSections.title: Court-ordered sealing - 943.059
sourceSections.text:
Florida sealing is usually the route for a case where adjudication was withheld, not where the person was adjudicated guilty.
Wilma must confirm:

Requirement Rule
Disposition Usually withhold of adjudication or other eligible non-conviction posture
Barred offense check Must not be ineligible under 943.0584
No adjudication for this case User must not have been adjudicated guilty/delinquent for acts tied to the arrest
Supervision complete User must no longer be under court supervision
No prior seal/expunge Generally no prior Florida court-ordered seal/expunge
Certificate FDLE Certificate of Eligibility required
Court discretion Court may deny even if FDLE issues certificate
The court cannot seal a record under 943.059 until the person first applies for and receives an FDLE certificate. Like expunction certificates, sealing certificates are valid for 12 months. (

"https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.059.html")

sourceSections.title: Floridas major disqualifiers - 943.0584
Automatic sealing - 943.0595
sourceSections.text:
Florida has an automatic sealing pathway for certain records that end favorably. FDLE automatically seals qualifying records after receiving the certified disposition from the clerk.
Automatic sealing may apply when:

Outcome Rule
No charging document filed Eligible if not excluded
Charging document filed, all counts dismissed/nolle prossed/dismissed by court Eligible if not excluded
Not guilty verdict on all counts Eligible, unless not guilty by reason of insanity
Judgment of acquittal on all counts Eligible
There is no limit on the number of automatic sealings under 943.0595, but automatic sealing does not require other criminal justice agencies to surrender or seal their own copies the same way a full court-ordered sealing/expungement order may. (

"https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&Search_String=&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0595.html")
Wilma rule: Automatic sealing is helpful, but do not substitute it blindly for court-ordered expunction. If the user wants maximum cleanup and qualifies for expunction, route to FDLE certificate + court petition.

sourceSections.title: Human trafficking victim expunction - 943.0583
sourceSections.text:
This is Floridas broadest special expunction route.
A human trafficking victim may petition to expunge criminal history records from arrests or charges for one or more offenses committed or reported while they were a victim and as part of the trafficking scheme or at the direction of a trafficker. This route applies
without regard to the disposition
of the arrest or charges, meaning it can reach some convictions that normal sealing/expungement cannot. ("https://www.leg.state.fl.us/Statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0583.html")
Key rules:

Requirement Rule
Victim status User was a victim of human trafficking
Connection Offense was committed/reported as part of trafficking scheme or at traffickers direction
Filing location Any court in the circuit where arrested, if that court has jurisdiction over the offense class
Filing fee Clerk may not charge filing/service/copy fees
Multiple cases Clerk treats multiple eligible cases as a single petition
Standard Preponderance of evidence; official documentation helps
Documentation Official documentation creates a presumption, but is not required
Fee No clerk filing fee under this statute
Exceptions Does not apply to offenses listed in 775.084(1)(b)1
Official documentation is not required, but if the user has it, it creates a presumption that participation in the offense resulted from trafficking; without official documentation, the statute requires a clear-and-convincing-evidence showing. (

"https://www.leg.state.fl.us/Statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0583.html")
Wilma trauma rule: Ask softly. Do not require graphic details. Use:
Was this charge connected to trafficking, coercion, exploitation, survival circumstances, or someone forcing or directing you to do something connected to the case?

sourceSections.title: Lawful self-defense expunction - 943.0578
Juvenile diversion expunction - 943.0582
sourceSections.text:
A minor who successfully completed an authorized diversion program may qualify for juvenile diversion expunction. The diversion must be based on a misdemeanor or a felony that is not a forcible felony and not a felony involving manufacture, sale, purchase, transport, possession, or use of a firearm or weapon. ("https://www.leg.state.fl.us/Statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0582.html")
FDLE says this route requires a juvenile diversion application, a State Attorney/Statewide Prosecutor certified statement, proof of diversion completion, and fingerprints. Juvenile diversion expunction does not block the person from later seeking a normal court-ordered sealing or expunction if otherwise eligible. ("https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/seal-and-expunge-process/juvenile-diversion-expunction")

sourceSections.title: Early juvenile expunction - 943.0515
sourceSections.text:
Florida juvenile records often have their own retention/expunction rules. A person age 18 or older but under 21 may apply for early juvenile expunction if they have not been charged by the State Attorney with, or found to have committed, any criminal offense during the 5 years before the application date. Only offenses committed before age 18 are eligible under this early-juvenile route. ("https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0515.html")
FDLEs early juvenile process requires an application, State Attorney/Statewide Prosecutor certified statement, certified dispositions, fingerprints, and the $75 processing fee. ("https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/seal-and-expunge-process/early-juvenile-expungement")

sourceSections.title: Administrative expunction - mistaken or unlawful arrest
sourceSections.text:
Administrative expunction is for an arrest made contrary to law or by mistake. The arresting agency, sheriff, State Attorney, or the person arrested may pursue it through FDLE, but the persons application must be supported by the head of the arresting agency/designee or the State Attorney/designee. ("https://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0900-0999%2F0943%2FSections%2F0943.0581.html")
Wilma rule: This is not a normal
case dismissed
route. Use it only when the arrest itself was mistaken, unlawful, identity-error-related, or otherwise contrary to law.

sourceSections.title: Waiting-period table
sourceSections.text:
Florida does not work like states with simple
3 years misdemeanor / 5 years felony
waits for normal adult relief. Florida mostly turns on disposition, adjudication, offense type, prior record, supervision status, and certificate eligibility.

Route Waiting period
Court-ordered expunction after no-file/dismissal/acquittal No fixed statutory wait, but disposition must be final and user must be off supervision
Court-ordered sealing after withhold No fixed statutory wait, but user must be off supervision and otherwise eligible
Expunction after prior sealing Usually same record must have been sealed at least 10 years, unless the case fits the no-file/dismissal-before-trial/acquittal/not-guilty exception
FDLE Certificate of Eligibility Valid for 12 months
Early juvenile expunction Applicant age 18-20, with a 5-year clean period before application
Juvenile diversion expunction After successful completion of qualifying diversion
Human trafficking victim expunction No ordinary wait; due diligence after victimization has ceased or after seeking services, subject to safety concerns
Automatic sealing After clerk transmits eligible certified disposition to FDLE
FDLE currently states that certificate application processing is over 12 weeks, and applications are processed in order received. (

"https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/seal-and-expunge-process/frequently-asked-questions")

sourceSections.title: Filing workflow
Fees
sourceSections.text:
Item Amount / note
FDLE Certificate of Eligibility $75 nonrefundable
Court filing fee County-dependent; many clerks list around $42, but verify locally
Certified copies County-dependent
Certified disposition County-dependent
Human trafficking victim expunction Clerk may not charge filing/service/copy fees under 943.0583
FDLEs $75 fee must be paid by money order, cashiers check, or personal check; FDLE does not accept cash, gift cards, or temporary personal checks. (

"https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/seal-and-expunge-process/certificate-of-eligibility-instructions")

sourceSections.title: Wilma intake questions for Florida
sourceSections.text:
Ask in this order:
1
Was this an adult case or juvenile case?

2
What county was the arrest in?

3
Were you physically arrested, or was it a Notice to Appear?

4
What was the arrest date or Notice to Appear date?

5
What was the exact charge and statute?

6
How did the case end: no file, dismissed, nolle prosequi, not guilty, acquittal, withhold of adjudication, adjudicated guilty, diversion, or still pending?

7
Were all counts resolved the same way?

8
Did you plead guilty or no contest?

9
Was adjudication withheld or were you adjudicated guilty?

10
Are you still on probation, court supervision, diversion, payment plan, or any sentence condition?

11
Have you ever been adjudicated guilty of any criminal offense anywhere?

12
Have you ever had a Florida record sealed or expunged before?

13
Was the charge one of the barred offenses, especially domestic violence, sex offense, child offense, violent offense, drug trafficking, manufacturing controlled substances, burglary of dwelling, robbery, or firearm/registration-related offense?

14
Was the case connected to human trafficking, coercion, or exploitation?

15
Was the dismissal/no-file based on lawful self-defense?

16
Was the arrest a mistake or made contrary to law?

17
Do you have the certified disposition from the clerk?

17. Wilma
do not say eligible unless&
rules
Wilma should not say
you are eligible
unless all required facts are confirmed.
For normal expunction, confirm:
disposition fits no-file/dismissal/nolle/acquittal/not guilty;
no disqualifying adjudication;
not a barred 943.0584 offense;
supervision is complete;
no prior Florida court seal/expunge unless the same-record 10-year sealed exception applies;
FDLE certificate can be pursued.
For normal sealing, confirm:
adjudication was withheld or another eligible posture exists;
no adjudication of guilt;
no barred 943.0584 offense;
supervision complete;
no prior Florida court seal/expunge;
FDLE certificate can be pursued.
For human trafficking, do not make the user relive trauma. Confirm only enough to route:
user says trafficking/coercion/exploitation was involved;
offense was part of the trafficking scheme or at trafficker direction;
manual/legal review triggered.
For lawful self-defense, confirm:
prosecutor/court dismissed or did not file because of lawful self-defense;
prosecutor certification is realistic.

sourceSections.title: Agent-facing summary
sourceSections.text:
For Florida, train Wilma with this core logic:
Default first step: FDLE Certificate of Eligibility.Expunction: no-file, dismissed, nolle prosequi, acquittal, not guilty, or certain already-sealed records after 10 years.Sealing: mostly withhold-of-adjudication cases, if not barred.Normal conviction/adjudicated guilty: usually not eligible for normal sealing/expunction.Major exception: human trafficking victim expunction can reach records regardless of disposition if the offense was tied to trafficking.Automatic sealing: helps favorable outcomes but is not the same as full court-ordered expunction.Biggest Florida mistake: assuming
withhold
means expungeable. In Florida, a withhold usually means seal first, and some withholds are still barred by 943.0584.

===== SOURCE FILE: Florida-RecordSealing-Expunction-Agent-Reference.pdf =====

Florida Record Sealing & Expunction
Agent Training Reference

A complete operational knowledge base for clearing criminal records in Florida: the FDLE Certificate-of-Eligibility
process, court-ordered SEALING (§ 943.059) and EXPUNCTION (§ 943.0585), automatic sealing (§ 943.0595),
and the administrative, lawful-self-defense, human-trafficking, and juvenile relief routes — all under Chapter
943, Florida Statutes.

Coverage State of Florida (FDLE for the Certificate of Eligibility; the circuit/county court where the arrest
occurred for the order)

Primary statutes § 943.059 (sealing); § 943.0585 (expunction); § 943.0584 (disqualifying offenses); § 943.0595
(automatic sealing)

Controlling changes 2019 amendment grouping same-incident arrests; 2023 HB 593 automatic-sealing expansion (§
943.0595)

Currency Reflects law as of mid-2026, including the automatic-sealing expansion

VERIFY BEFORE RELYING
Florida draws two lines the agent must never blur. First, SEALING (§ 943.059) hides a record from the public while government
agencies still see it; EXPUNCTION (§ 943.0585) destroys it (FDLE keeps only a confidential notation) and the person may
generally deny it occurred. Second, almost all relief is a TWO-STEP process: the person must first obtain a Certificate of
Eligibility from FDLE ($75, valid 12 months), and THEN petition the court — and the certificate does NOT guarantee the court
will grant the order. Relief is generally ONCE PER LIFETIME. The disqualifying-offense list in § 943.0584 bars relief even
when adjudication was withheld. The agent must verify the current statute, the § 943.0584 list, and eligibility before relying.

How the agent should use this document
Florida clears adult records by SEALING or EXPUNCTION, and the agent routes by disposition and goal. The
threshold question is adjudication: if the person was ADJUDICATED GUILTY of anything, anywhere, the record
is generally ineligible. If adjudication was WITHHELD (and the offense is not on the § 943.0584 list), the record
can be SEALED; if all charges were DISMISSED / dropped / not filed (and the state attorney certifies for
expunction), the record can be EXPUNGED. The agent should (1) confirm no adjudication of guilt and no prior
seal/expunge; (2) check the § 943.0584 disqualifier list; (3) determine seal vs. expunge by disposition; and (4)
run the FDLE Certificate-of-Eligibility step before the court petition.

CONTENTS

sourceSections.title: Sample petitions and completed examples
sourceSections.text:
Statutory shorthand used throughout

Term Meaning

Sealing (§ 943.059) Record hidden from public; government/criminal-justice agencies retain access; available
where adjudication was withheld

Expunction (§ 943.0585) Record physically destroyed; FDLE keeps a confidential notation; available where charges
were dismissed/dropped (state attorney must certify)

Certificate of Eligibility (COE) FDLE document confirming statutory eligibility to petition the court; $75, valid 12 months;
required FIRST step

Withhold of adjudication Court withholds a formal conviction; NOT a conviction, and the gateway to sealing

Adjudication of guilt A conviction; one anywhere generally bars sealing and expunction

Automatic sealing (§ Sealing by operation of law when the clerk submits a qualifying disposition; same effect as
943.0595) court-ordered sealing

§ 943.0584 list Statutory disqualifying offenses (sexual, violent, trafficking, etc.) that bar relief even with a
withhold

FDLE Florida Department of Law Enforcement — issues the COE and maintains the state criminal
history

sourceSections.title: Eligibility rules
sourceSections.text:
Routed by disposition, with several distinct relief types under Chapter 943.

Court-ordered sealing (§ 943.059). Available where adjudication was WITHHELD (no conviction), the person
has never been adjudicated guilty of any offense (or delinquent for a felony/specified misdemeanor) in any
jurisdiction, has no prior seal/expunge, and the offense is not on the § 943.0584 list. Sealing hides the record
from the public; government agencies retain access.

Court-ordered expunction (§ 943.0585). Available where the charges were DISMISSED, dropped, not filed, or
otherwise resolved without adjudication AND the state attorney/statewide prosecutor certifies eligibility, OR
where a record has been sealed for 10 years and is otherwise eligible. Expunction destroys the record; FDLE
retains only a confidential notation. Same lifetime and § 943.0584 limits apply.

Other relief types. ADMINISTRATIVE expunction (§ 943.0581) for an arrest made contrary to law or by
mistake; LAWFUL SELF-DEFENSE expunction (§ 943.0578) where the state attorney certifies self-defense and
charges were not filed or were dismissed; HUMAN-TRAFFICKING expunction (§ 943.0583) for victims;
AUTOMATIC sealing (§ 943.0595) by operation of law; and JUVENILE routes (§§ 943.0515, 943.0582 diversion
expunction).

Florida Record Sealing & Expunction — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase

AGENT GUIDANCE
Start with adjudication: ANY adjudication of guilt (anywhere, ever) generally bars sealing AND expunction. A WITHHOLD
opens sealing; a DISMISSAL opens expunction.
Relief is generally ONCE PER LIFETIME under §§ 943.059 / 943.0585 — confirm the person has never sealed or expunged a
Florida record before.

sourceSections.title: Waiting periods
sourceSections.text:
Florida’s gate is disposition and completion of supervision, not a fixed conviction-free clock; the one true waiting
period is the 10-year seal-then-expunge bridge.

Disposition / route Remedy Timing

Charges dismissed / dropped / not filed Expunction (§ 943.0585) After supervision complete; SA
certifies

Adjudication withheld (not § 943.0584) Sealing (§ 943.059) After supervision complete

Record sealed under § 943.059 Then expunction After 10 years sealed

Arrest contrary to law / by mistake Administrative expunction (§ On FDLE application
943.0581)

Lawful self-defense, no charges/dismissed Self-defense expunction (§ On SA certification
943.0578)

Qualifying disposition (dismissal/acquittal) Automatic sealing (§ 943.0595) By operation of law

Certificate of Eligibility (FDLE) Prerequisite to petition Valid 12 months once issued

There is no general conviction-free waiting period because Florida relief turns on the absence of an adjudication
of guilt rather than the passage of time. The person must have completed all court supervision for the arrest at
issue. The signature waiting period is the 10-year bridge: a record initially ineligible for expunction (because
adjudication was withheld) may become expungeable after it has been SEALED for 10 years. The FDLE
Certificate of Eligibility, once issued, is valid for 12 months.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Don’t look for a fixed conviction-free clock — the gate is ‘no adjudication of guilt’ plus completed supervision. The one real wait
is the 10-year seal-then-expunge bridge.
The COE expires 12 months after issuance — file the court petition within that window or the certificate must be renewed.

sourceSections.title: Disqualifying / ineligible offenses
sourceSections.text:
Two kinds of bar: a record-level bar (any adjudication of guilt) and an offense-level bar (the § 943.0584 list).

Record-level bars. Any adjudication of guilt as an adult for any criminal offense or comparable ordinance
violation (in any jurisdiction); any juvenile adjudication of delinquency for a felony or specified misdemeanor;
incomplete court supervision; a prior Florida seal/expunge; or being a defendant in a pending case.

Offense-level bars (§ 943.0584) — ineligible even when adjudication was withheld:

Florida Record Sealing & Expunction — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase

Sexual offenses and offenses requiring sex-offender/predator registration (e.g., Ch. 794, §§ 800.04, 827.071,
847.0133/0135/0145).
Violent and trafficking offenses enumerated under § 907.041 and drug trafficking (§ 893.135).
Abuse of an elderly person or disabled adult (§ 825.1025); offenses against vulnerable persons.
Acts of domestic violence, child abuse, kidnapping/false imprisonment of a minor (§ 787.025), and related
enumerated crimes.
Communications-fraud / scheme offenses (§ 817.034) and other specifically listed statutes.

VERIFY BEFORE RELYING
The § 943.0584 disqualifying-offense list is long and enumerated by statute section, and it bars sealing and expunction even
after a 10-year seal and even where adjudication was withheld. The agent must map the specific offense to its statute and
confirm it is not on the § 943.0584 list, in addition to checking the record-level bars, before declaring eligibility.

sourceSections.title: Case outcome rules
sourceSections.text:
Disposition determines whether the route is sealing, expunction, or neither. This is the agent's primary routing
logic for Florida.

Case outcome Route Channel Note

Dismissed / nolle prosequi / not Expunction (§ 943.0585) FDLE COE → court SA certifies
filed

Acquittal (not guilty) Expunction; auto-seal Court / § 943.0595 If not § 943.0584

Adjudication withheld Sealing (§ 943.059) FDLE COE → court If not § 943.0584

Sealed 10 years Expunction FDLE COE → court If otherwise eligible

Arrest by mistake / contrary to Administrative expunction FDLE (§ 943.0581) No court needed
law

Lawful self-defense Self-defense expunction FDLE (§ 943.0578) SA certifies

Adjudicated guilty (any Not eligible — Record-level bar
jurisdiction)

Offense on § 943.0584 list Not eligible — Even with withhold

Effect of relief: a SEALED record is removed from public view but remains accessible to criminal-justice and
certain licensing agencies; an EXPUNGED record is destroyed by all agencies, with FDLE keeping only a
confidential notation that a record existed, and the person may lawfully deny the arrest in most circumstances
(with narrow exceptions for law-enforcement employment, Bar admission, and certain licensing). Multiple arrests
from the SAME incident may be grouped in one petition at the court’s discretion.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Match disposition to route: dismissal → expunction; withhold → sealing; guilty → neither. Then screen against § 943.0584.
Tell users plainly: sealing hides, expunction destroys; both are once per lifetime; same-incident arrests can sometimes be
grouped.

sourceSections.title: Required forms
sourceSections.text:
Florida uses an FDLE application first, then court pleadings under the Rules of Criminal Procedure.

Form / channel Use

FDLE Application for Certificate of Eligibility The required first step; submitted with fingerprints, a certified disposition,
and the $75 fee. State attorney completes Section B for expunction.

Certificate of Eligibility (issued by FDLE) FDLE’s determination of statutory eligibility; valid 12 months; must
accompany the court petition.

Petition to Seal / Expunge Court pleading under Fla. R. Crim. P. 3.692 and § 943.059 / § 943.0585,
filed in the circuit/county court where the arrest occurred.

Affidavit in support Sworn affidavit accompanying the petition, attesting to eligibility.

Proposed Order to Seal / Expunge Fla. R. Crim. P. 3.989 form order for the judge’s signature.

The FDLE application page must be completed in full (name, DOB, race, sex, addresses, arresting agency, date
of arrest, charges) and include fingerprints and a certified disposition. For EXPUNCTION the state attorney or
statewide prosecutor must complete the certification section; if it is not completed, FDLE processes the request
as a SEALING. After the COE issues, the petition, affidavit, and proposed order are filed with the court.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Sequence matters: FDLE application + fingerprints + certified disposition + $75 FIRST; the court petition, affidavit, and
proposed order come only AFTER the COE issues.
For expunction, make sure the state attorney completes the certification — without it FDLE downgrades the request to a
sealing.

sourceSections.title: Filing instructions
sourceSections.text: Florida relief is a strict two-step: FDLE Certificate of Eligibility, then a court petition.

sourceSections.title: Confirm eligibility. No adjudication of guilt anywhere; supervision complete; no prior seal/expunge; offense
sourceSections.text:
not on the § 943.0584 list; correct route (seal vs. expunge) by disposition.
2. Get the disposition and prints. Obtain a certified copy of the final disposition and a fingerprint card.

sourceSections.title: FDLE application. Complete the FDLE Application for a Certificate of Eligibility; for expunction have the
sourceSections.text:
state attorney complete the certification; submit with the certified disposition, fingerprints, and $75 fee.
4. Receive the COE. FDLE issues the Certificate of Eligibility (valid 12 months). The COE confirms eligibility to
petition — it is NOT itself the sealing/expunction order.

sourceSections.title: File the court petition. File the Petition to Seal/Expunge, sworn affidavit, the COE, and a proposed order in
sourceSections.text:
the circuit/county court where the arrest occurred, under Fla. R. Crim. P. 3.692.
6. Court decision. The court may grant or deny in its discretion even with a valid COE; on a granted order,
FDLE and other agencies seal or destroy the records.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Drill the two-step: a Certificate of Eligibility is necessary but NOT sufficient — the court can still deny.
Venue is the court where the arrest occurred; file within the COE’s 12-month validity window.

sourceSections.title: Court-specific quirks
sourceSections.text:
The features most likely to surprise.

Seal vs. expunge is the core distinction. Sealing hides (government still sees); expunction destroys (FDLE
keeps a confidential notation). Disposition decides which: withhold → seal, dismissal → expunge.
Two-step process. FDLE Certificate of Eligibility FIRST, then the court petition. The COE does not guarantee
the order — the judge has discretion.
Once per lifetime. Generally one seal or expunge per person under §§ 943.059 / 943.0585; same-incident
arrests may be grouped at the court’s discretion.
Any adjudication of guilt is fatal. A single conviction anywhere, ever (even unrelated), bars relief. A
WITHHOLD is not a conviction.
The § 943.0584 list bars relief even with a withhold — and even after a 10-year seal.
The 10-year bridge. A withhold-of-adjudication record can be sealed, then expunged after 10 years sealed if
otherwise eligible.
Automatic sealing exists (§ 943.0595) for qualifying dismissals/acquittals by operation of law — but verify it
actually occurred.
Criminal traffic counts. DUI, reckless driving, and certain DWLS are criminal and can disqualify; a DUI
conviction itself is not sealable/expungeable.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Lead every Florida analysis with two screens: (1) any adjudication of guilt anywhere? and (2) is the offense on the § 943.0584
list? Those two decide most cases before timing even matters.
Frame relief as two steps and once-per-lifetime so users plan the single shot carefully.

sourceSections.title: Fee information
sourceSections.text:
The core cost is the FDLE processing fee; court and certified-copy costs vary by county.

Item Amount Note

FDLE Certificate of Eligibility $75 Money order to FDLE; executive director may
waive

Automatic sealing (§ 943.0595) $0 By operation of law; no application

Administrative expunction (§ 943.0581) Typically no FDLE fee Arrest by mistake / contrary to law

Certified disposition / fingerprints Varies Clerk and law-enforcement fees

Court filing Varies by county Some petitions filed without a separate fee

Attorney fees (if represented) Varies Not required; clinics and legal aid assist

Florida Record Sealing & Expunction — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase

VERIFY BEFORE RELYING
The $75 FDLE fee, county court costs, and certified-copy/fingerprint fees can change, and fee waivers are available in limited
circumstances; the agent should verify the current FDLE fee and any county court costs at the time of advising rather than
quoting fixed figures from memory.

sourceSections.title: Criminal history (FDLE) request process
sourceSections.text:
Reviewing the record first confirms each disposition, whether adjudication was withheld, and whether any
offense is on the § 943.0584 list.

A. State history — FDLE. The Florida Department of Law Enforcement maintains the official state criminal
history and issues the Certificate of Eligibility. A Personal Review of one’s own FDLE record confirms
dispositions before applying.

B. Court / clerk record. The clerk of the circuit court supplies the certified disposition the FDLE application
requires and is where the petition is later filed.

C. Federal — FBI Identity History Summary. For records that may extend beyond Florida, the FBI
fingerprint-based summary is the federal equivalent; Florida relief reaches Florida records only.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Order a certified disposition from the clerk and (if useful) an FDLE Personal Review before applying — they confirm the
withhold-vs-adjudication status the whole analysis turns on.

sourceSections.title: Sample petitions and completed examples
sourceSections.text:
Florida relief is filed on court pleadings after the FDLE certificate issues. Names, dates, and identifiers below are
fictional and illustrative.

Example A — Petition to Seal (Fla. R. Crim. P. 3.692; § 943.059)
IN THE CIRCUIT COURT, FOURTH JUDICIAL CIRCUIT, DUVAL COUNTY, FL
IN RE: THE PETITION OF MARIA T. ALVAREZ Case No. 16-2018-CF-XXXXXX
PETITION TO SEAL CRIMINAL HISTORY RECORD (943.059)
1. Petitioner: Maria T. Alvarez; DOB 11/30/1990.
2. Petitioner was arrested on 08/14/2018 by the Jacksonville
Sheriff's Office for possession of a controlled substance.
3. Adjudication was WITHHELD; petitioner was never adjudicated
guilty of this or any offense in any jurisdiction.
4. The offense is NOT listed in s. 943.0584.
5. Petitioner has completed all court supervision and has no
prior sealing or expunction in Florida.

sourceSections.title: AUTOMATIC SEALING (943.0595):
sourceSections.text:
When the clerk submits a qualifying certified disposition
(e.g., all counts dismissed or an acquittal), the FDLE
record is sealed by operation of law -- no application,
same effect as a court-ordered seal. Verify it occurred.

This reference is an internal training and retrieval aid for the Wilma assistant. It summarizes Florida statutes and common practice
FDLE-then-court process, with automatic sealing under § 943.0595 and a disqualifying-offense list in § 943.0584. The agent must
verify the current statutory text, the § 943.0584 list, the eligibility and once-per-lifetime rules, the FDLE Certificate-of-Eligibility process
and fee, and the court’s discretion before relying on any rule here.
